item.title: clawhq evolve CLI command is a stub — prints 'not implemented' instead of managing capabilities
item.intent: clawhq evolve is the top-level command for the 'agent grows' pillar. It must orchestrate the sub-systems (skills, tools, integrations, memory) into a coherent capability management experience.
item.description: src/cli/index.ts lines 2358-2365: the 'evolve' command checks installation then prints 'Not yet implemented. Coming soon.' and exits. All sub-systems exist as separate commands (clawhq skill, clawhq tool, clawhq integrate, clawhq memory) but the top-level 'evolve' command that ties them together is a stub. This may be a UX question: should 'clawhq evolve' be an interactive dashboard showing current capabilities with options to add/remove, or should it just list sub-commands?
steps: Decide UX: interactive capability dashboard vs sub-command listing vs guided wizard
If dashboard: show current skills, tools, integrations, memory status in a single view with action prompts
If sub-command listing: print available evolve sub-commands with descriptions
Implement chosen approach in CLI handler
Run npm test to confirm no regression
acceptanceCriteria: clawhq evolve does not print 'not implemented'
clawhq evolve provides a useful entry point for capability management
All existing sub-commands (skill, tool, integrate, memory) continue to work independently
